sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 67

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 67:1
items.html:
(to the question) Mechitzot [partitions]
The Kelim — that is, the Guf of the Partzuf — come from the spreading of the Masach of that Partzuf; therefore every Partzuf is bounded with partitions of its own, that is, according to the measure of the Aviut in the Masach, whether of Behina Aleph or of Behina Bet and so forth. A Partzuf of Behina Aleph, for example, cannot receive anything from a Partzuf of Behina Bet, because the partitions of Behina Bet in the Kelim of its Guf hold it back. And the whole difference between the degrees is recognized only in these partitions — that is, in them govern the limits and the Aviut that branch out from the discernment of the Masach of that Partzuf. (p. 777, Items 67 and 68) The sum of the twenty-two letters of each Partzuf is found in the Yesod of that Partzuf — that is, in the Masach there, from which its twenty-two letters were born and came out. And they are drawn there in the form of a single Dalet, in the secret of door and hinge (see Answer 28), where the hinge contains two Heys joined together, from which two Vavs spread out. There is a first hinge and door of ZA, from the time of Katnut; and there is a second hinge and door from the side of Ima, from the time of Gadlut. And at the time of Gadlut, when she attains the second door and hinge, the two doors join one another in the form of a closed Mem, which closes about the embryo so that it cannot go out from there. And this closing force is in the Vavs, which are the discernment of the Yesod itself. For now, at the time of Gadlut, when the lower Hey has already descended from the hinge — which is the secret of the Tzimtzum and the force of Din that were there — no Aviut remains there any longer except from the Yesod itself, from its root in the Yesod of AB; and it ends all the Lights in the Nukva, even the discernments of Hassadim in her, in the secret of its force being drawn from the Yesod of AB, while "Ima spreads only as far as Hod." And the Lights of Hassadim of Ima stop, and do not touch it. Therefore it closes, from itself and upward, in the Yesod of Ima, so that not even the least of the Hassadim pass through it. And for this it is called a closed Mem: the four Vavs there join together and seal off the Hassadim from the Yesod of Ima onward, that they spread not even a little. And since all this closure is made by the force of the illumination of the Yesod of AB — in whose Kli neither the discernment of Katnut of the lower Hey in the Eynaim nor the discernment of the breaking governed, as above in Answer 28 — it is therefore a most excellent guard against the forces of Din and the outer ones, which have no possibility whatever of sucking from the forces of Din that are there in the newborn itself. Understand this. (Item 87)
